ncs_standard_definition.description_short: Designs and plans exhibitions, events, and displays to engage audiences and convey messages.
ncs_standard_definition.description_detailed: Develops concepts, themes, and visual elements for exhibitions, events, and displays, considering factors like audience demographics, space constraints, and budget limitations. Utilizes creativity, technical skills, and project management expertise to bring designs to life and ensure successful execution.

first_stepper.description: High school students interested in art, design, and event management can pursue this career.
target_degrees: B.Des in Exhibition Design or related fields

choice_maker.description: Individuals with a generic degree in design or a related field can transition into exhibition design with additional training.

future_builder.description: Experienced exhibition designers can advance their careers by developing expertise in specific areas, such as digital exhibitions or event management.
future_builder.prerequisite_experience: 2+ years of experience as an Exhibition Designer

problem_solver_uturn.description: Individuals from unrelated fields can transition into exhibition design by acquiring relevant skills and experience.
